title: campylanthus glaber
intro: Campylanthus glaber, also known as the hairy-leaved campylanthus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, campylanthus glaber is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to benefit from its health properties, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
